data.title: Planet Morning News

data.title: The US SOL spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $7.5964 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the SOL spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $7.5964 million yesterday (March 13th, Eastern Time). Only the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) saw net inflows yesterday, with a single-day net inflow of $7.5964 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $782 million. As of press time, the SOL spot ETF has a total net asset value of $855 million, a net asset value ratio of 1.68%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $969 million.

data.title: Iran Warns of Retaliation Against Attacks on Its Oil Infrastructure
data.description: Iran's Joint Armed Forces Command has issued a warning that any attacks on its oil and energy infrastructure will lead to retaliatory actions against the energy infrastructure owned by oil companies cooperating with the United States in the region. According to Jin10, this statement underscores the heightened tensions in the region, as Iran seeks to protect its vital energy assets. The warning comes amid ongoing geopolitical conflicts and highlights the potential risks to global energy markets. The situation remains tense, with implications for international relations and energy security.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stocks Close Mixed as UPXI Surges Over 19%
data.description: U.S. stocks ended the trading session with mixed results, as crypto-related stock UPXI experienced a significant surge of over 19.3%. According to NS3.AI, the Dow Jones Industrial Average decreased by 0.26%, while the S&P 500 saw a decline of 0.61%. The Nasdaq also closed lower, reflecting a day of varied performance across major indices.

data.title: Meta Plans Significant Layoffs Amid AI Infrastructure Costs
data.description:
Meta is reportedly planning substantial layoffs that could affect 20% or more of its workforce. According to Odaily, this move is part of Meta's strategy to offset the high costs associated with its artificial intelligence infrastructure and to enhance the efficiency of AI-assisted employees.

Sources indicate that the exact date and scale of the layoffs have not been finalized. If the layoffs reach the projected 20%, it would mark the most significant reduction in staff since the company's restructuring at the end of 2022 and the beginning of 2023, which was termed the 'Year of Efficiency.'

data.title: U.S. President Trump Highlights Divergence in U.S.-Israel Goals on Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has acknowledged that the United States and Israel may have slightly different objectives regarding Iran. According to Jin10, Trump emphasized the nuanced differences in the strategic goals of the two nations concerning Iran's activities in the region. This statement comes amid ongoing discussions and diplomatic efforts to address Iran's nuclear program and regional influence. The U.S. and Israel have historically collaborated closely on security matters, but Trump's remarks suggest a potential divergence in their approaches to handling Iran's geopolitical challenges.

data.title: Japan Hosts Indo-Pacific Energy Security Forum Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: Japan's Minister for Economic Revitalization, Ryo Akizawa, announced the commencement of the 'Indo-Pacific Energy Security Forum' in Tokyo over the next two days. According to Jin10, the event is co-hosted by the Japanese and U.S. governments, bringing together ministers and industry leaders from various countries. The forum aims to foster bilateral discussions and cooperation, which are particularly crucial given the current tensions in the Middle East.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin interprets the Ethereum Foundation's mission statement: Ethereum should be a "safe haven for technology."
data.description: In a social media post interpreting the Ethereum Foundation's new mission statement, the "EF Mandate," Vitalik Buterin stated that Ethereum plays a unique role in the global digital ecosystem, aiming to become a "technology haven" to maintain technological self-sovereignty and promote collaboration in an environment free from coercion and domination. He emphasized that Ethereum aims to ensure that no individual, organization, or ideology can gain absolute control in cyberspace and provides users with an exit mechanism. Vitalik explained that the Ethereum Foundation will continue to advance the CROPS principles—censorship and capture resistance, open source, privacy, and security—prioritizing decentralization, verifiability, network activity, and security at the protocol layer, and driving upgrades including account abstraction to reduce reliance on intermediaries. Simultaneously, the foundation will promote user experiences that balance security, privacy, and user autonomy at the application layer and collaborate with ecosystem projects to advance Ethereum's development. Vitalik stated that he will double down on his commitment to Ethereum. Previous news: The Ethereum Foundation releases the "EF Mandate," emphasizing self-sovereignty and decentralization principles.

data.title: Dubai's Financial Hub Status Challenged Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Dubai's reputation as a secure and stable international financial center is facing challenges due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that if wealthy individuals in Asia consider relocating their assets, Singapore might become a potential alternative. The situation underscores the geopolitical risks that can impact financial hubs and influence the decisions of affluent investors seeking stability for their investments.

data.title: Canada to Increase Oil and Natural Gas Exports Amid Global Energy Market Disruptions
data.description: Canada is set to deliver 23.6 million barrels of oil and boost natural gas exports in the upcoming months to support global energy markets affected by the Iran conflict. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Canada's role in the international initiative aimed at stabilizing energy supplies. This move comes as countries worldwide seek to mitigate the impact of geopolitical tensions on energy availability and prices. The increased exports are part of a broader strategy to ensure market stability and address potential shortages. Canada's commitment underscores its position as a key player in the global energy sector, contributing to efforts to maintain equilibrium in the face of ongoing disruptions.

data.title: US stocks closed mixed for crypto-related stocks, with UPXI rising over 19.3%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.26%, the S&P 500 fell 0.61%, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.93%. Cryptocurrency stocks showed mixed performance, with UPXI rising over 19.3%, HODL rising 7.04%, MARA rising 6.39%, and HOOD falling 3.59%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Blockchain Capital is suspected of transferring 216,000 AAVE tokens to Coinbase Prime, worth approximately $24.8 million.
data.description: According to crypto analyst EmberCN, investment firm Blockchain Capital has transferred all of its 216,000 AAVE tokens to Coinbase Prime, worth approximately $24.8 million. The firm had previously purchased approximately 233,000 AAVE tokens between October 2020 and March 2023, at an average price of around $94, and had long been one of the major institutional holders of AAVE. Previously, near the AAVE price peak in December 2024, Blockchain Capital sold 16,900 AAVE tokens through FalconX, worth approximately $5.04 million. With this transfer, its early holdings have essentially been divested.

data.title: Energy Supply Crunch Threatens Global Food Crop Production
data.description: Food crops are increasingly at risk due to the energy supply crunch exacerbated by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that farmers across Asia and Europe are experiencing a shortage of fuel necessary for operating vital agricultural machinery. This scarcity poses a significant threat to food production, as the availability of energy resources is crucial for the cultivation and harvesting of crops. The situation underscores the interconnectedness of global energy markets and agricultural output, with potential implications for food security worldwide.

data.title: The Ethereum Foundation released the "EF Mandate," emphasizing the principles of self-sovereignty and decentralization.
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation (ETH) released the "EF Mandate," serving as its "charter, manifesto, and action guide," clarifying its mission, decision-making principles, and role within the Ethereum ecosystem. The document states that Ethereum's core goal is to guarantee user self-sovereignty, meaning users have ultimate control over their identity, assets, and actions. The Foundation emphasizes that Ethereum's development must always adhere to four core attributes: censorship resistance, open source and freedom, privacy, and security (collectively known as CROPS), stating that these principles should not be sacrificed for efficiency or convenience. It also places Ethereum within a broader open technology ecosystem, referring to it as part of the "Infinite Garden," an open digital infrastructure network jointly built by developers, the community, and institutions. The ETH Foundation states that its role is that of a "guardian" of the project, not an owner or ruler, and its responsibility is to maintain the network's long-term vision and open values. The Foundation also emphasizes that as the ecosystem matures, its influence should gradually decrease, with more responsibilities being assumed by the broader community and developer ecosystem.

data.title: CFTC Chairman: The CFTC is setting standards and recruiting talent for cryptocurrencies, AI, and prediction markets.
data.description: CFTC Chairman Mike Selig stated in a post on the X platform that the CFTC is developing applicable rules and standards for major innovations in the golden age of US financial markets, including cryptocurrencies, AI, and prediction markets. It is seeking top talent in the technology and marketing fields to help the agency navigate into the future.

data.title: U.S. Interior Secretary Discusses Oil Futures Market Strategy
data.description: U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum has revealed that American officials have considered using the oil futures market as a strategy to curb the surge in crude oil prices. According to Jin10, this discussion highlights the ongoing efforts by the U.S. government to manage energy costs and stabilize the market amid fluctuating global oil prices. The strategy aims to address the economic impact of rising oil prices on both consumers and industries. This approach reflects the broader governmental measures being evaluated to ensure energy affordability and market stability.

data.title: South Korean Prime Minister Addresses U.S. Trade Concerns
data.description: South Korean Prime Minister has commented on recent statements made by U.S. Trade Representative Greer, indicating that South Korea may not be the target of a Section 301 investigation under U.S. trade law. According to Jin10, the remarks come amid ongoing discussions about international trade relations and potential investigations that could impact South Korea's economic interactions with the United States. The Prime Minister's statement aims to clarify South Korea's position and alleviate concerns regarding potential trade disputes. This development is part of broader efforts to maintain stable trade relations between the two countries.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. Commerce Department Withdraws AI Chip Export Regulation
data.description: The U.S. Commerce Department has withdrawn a proposed regulation that aimed to limit the export of artificial intelligence chips globally without prior approval from the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this decision in the context of international trade and technology development. The draft regulation was initially intended to control the distribution of advanced AI technology, reflecting concerns over national security and technological competitiveness. The move to retract the regulation suggests a reconsideration of the balance between innovation and security in the AI sector. This development is likely to impact the global AI chip market, influencing both manufacturers and consumers worldwide.

data.title: Sources: Meta may lay off at least 20% of its staff.
data.description: According to foreign media citing sources, Meta plans a large-scale layoff that could affect 20% or more of its workforce, as Meta seeks to offset its expensive bets on artificial intelligence infrastructure and prepare for improving the efficiency of AI-assisted employees. The sources stated that the date and scale of the layoffs are undetermined. If the layoffs reach 20%, it will be Meta's most significant round of layoffs since its restructuring, dubbed the "Year of Efficiency," in late 2022 and early 2023. (Jinshi)

data.title: FAA Lifts Ground Stop Orders at Washington's Major Airports
data.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has announced the lifting of ground stop orders at three major airports in the Washington area. According to Jin10, the directive was initially implemented due to unspecified reasons affecting air traffic control operations. The affected airports included Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport, Washington Dulles International Airport, and Baltimore/Washington International Thurgood Marshall Airport. The FAA has not provided further details on the cause of the disruption or the duration of the ground stop. Travelers are advised to check with airlines for updated flight information.
